name: Cofrentes a Fuente de Casas del Río — circular por La Chirrichana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cofrentes offers diverse terrain shaped by the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, which have carved dramatic gorges and canyons. The landscape features a unique volcanic element with the Cerro de Agras, an extinct volcano providing panoramic views. Riders can explore mountainous areas with varied relief, including ravines and peaks, enveloped by Mediterranean forests. The region's natural features provide a comprehensive network of mountain bike trails Cofrentes, suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cofrentes The most popular mountain bike trail is Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance Trail, a 16.6 miles (26.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 50 minutes to complete, winding through varied terrain near the river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Alto de Alcola MTB Route, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, offering expansive views of the surrounding valleys and hills. Local mountain bikers also love the Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop, a 43.2 miles (69.6 km) trail leading through river canyons and volcanic landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Cofrentes is defined by dramatic river canyons, unique volcanic topography, and lush Mediterranean forests. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easier riverside paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Cofrentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. More than 80 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cofrentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cofrentes?
A: Cofrentes offers a diverse range of terrain for mountain biking. You'll encounter dramatic gorges and canyons carved by the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, as well as unique volcanic landscapes around the Cerro de Agras, an extinct volcano. The trails also wind through mountainous areas with ravines, peaks, and lush Mediterranean forests featuring pine, rosemary, and juniper.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, Cofrentes has options for various skill levels. While some routes require good fitness and advanced riding skills, there are also circular routes of lower difficulty that are suitable for families or those new to mountain biking. For example, routes along the Cabriel River offer easier, scenic paths.

Q: Where can I find trails with great views in Cofrentes?
A: Many mountain bike trails in Cofrentes are designed to maximize scenic enjoyment. Routes that ascend to the Volcán del Cerro Agrás offer magnificent panoramic views of Cofrentes, its Castle, and the Júcar and Cabriel rivers. Trails winding along the rivers and through breathtaking gorges also provide stunning vistas.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Cofrentes?
A: Cofrentes is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the dramatic Cañones del Júcar, the unique Cerro de Agras volcano, and the confluence of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers. Notable landmarks include the historic Cofrentes Castle, perched atop a volcanic chimney, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3216823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de Cortes Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, Cofrentes features several circular mountain bike routes. These are often highlighted for their scenic value and can range in difficulty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336800910/cofrentes-a-fuente-de-casas-del-rio-circular-por-la-chirrichana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes a Fuente de Casas del Río — circular por La Chirrichana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 40 km loop that offers a great experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Cofrentes?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cofrentes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic river canyons, the unique volcanic topography, and the lush Mediterranean forests that define the region's diverse terrain. The variety of routes, from easier riverside paths to challenging climbs, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails in Cofrentes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Cofrentes offers several challenging routes that demand good fitness and advanced riding skills. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910755316/cofrentes-and-cortes-de-pallas-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofrentes and Cortes de Pallás MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult 69.6 km trail leading through river canyons and volcanic landscapes, often taking over six hours to complete.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that follow the rivers in Cofrentes?
A: Many trails in Cofrentes are designed to follow the course of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, offering picturesque views and often less technical riding. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336254047/las-aldeas-de-basta-y-alcance-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that winds through varied terrain near the river, providing a great riverside experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Cofrentes?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the Mediterranean climate of Cofrentes generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if riding during this season.

Q: Are there any facilities or shelters along the mountain bike routes in Cofrentes?
A: Yes, the region has some facilities that can be useful for mountain bikers. You might find forest shelters like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4288173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Manquillo Forest Shelter&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3322408" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest Shelter with Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3893031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Tobarro Recreation Area&lt;/a&gt; also offers facilities for a break.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Cofrentes area?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails available in the Cofrentes area, catering to various skill levels. This includes a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every mountain biking enthusiast.

Q: Are there any trails that offer significant elevation gain for a good workout?
A: For riders looking for a significant climb and a challenging workout, trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1336363785/el-alto-de-alcola-mtb-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Alto de Alcola MTB Route&lt;/a&gt; are an excellent choice. This moderate 24.7 km path features substantial elevation gain, rewarding riders with expansive views of the surrounding valleys and hills.
tips:
Cofrentes sports center parking.

The Cofrentes town council makes the sports center available free of charge for cyclists who take routes in the area. In addition to a bike washing area, basic tools and an information point with more routes.

It is the perfect point for the start and end of the route.

If you are going on a route of several days, it is better that you let the sports center staff know of your plans so that they do not worry about seeing the car stopped there. Remember to let one or more people know about your route so that, in the event of an accident, it will be easier to locate you.

Fountain on the side of the road and down some stairs. The water is collected in a type of bathtub from which it comes out through a jet.

The water is untreated and in this case it is preferable to do so before drinking it.
A beautiful natural viewpoint with incredible views.
There is a fountain with four taps in the very heart of the village, where fresh, treated water flows constantly, making it a refreshing and welcoming spot for locals and visitors alike.
At Casas del Rio you can see this hydraulic water wheel right by the bridge over the Rio Cabriel.
Quiet place with tables, pier, fountain and garbage. Beautiful views of the town of Cofrentes.
